Now, how does this relate to other Eastern concepts? Zen Buddhism comes to mind. Zen emphasizes simplicity, mindfulness, and the present moment. Maybe wabi-sabi is a part of that. Then there's the concept of 'mono no aware', which is the awareness of the impermanence of things. That seems related because wabi-sabi is about impermanence. Maybe they are connected. Also, 'mujo' (無常), which means impermanence, is a key concept in Buddhism. So wabi-sabi might be a manifestation of that idea.
